synthesis.target_compound: Pt/OLC (Pt nanoparticles decorated on onion-like carbon)

steps.description: Nanodiamond (ND) powder was annealed at 1500°C to convert it to onion-like carbon (OLC).
The annealed OLC was oxidized by mixed acid to functionalize the surface with oxygenated functional groups.
OLC was mixed with chloroplatinic acid solution in water without any additional reductant or surfactant.

Hydrothermal treatment of the OLC and chloroplatinic acid mixture at 200°C without any additional reductant or surfactant. OLC acts as both reductant and surfactant, reducing chloroplatinic acid to form Pt nanoparticles decorated on OLC.

synthesis.notes: OLC acts as both reductant and surfactant during the hydrothermal step, reducing chloroplatinic acid to form Pt nanoparticles (1-2 nm diameter) on OLC (~5 nm diameter) without any additional reductant or surfactant. The C-O functional groups on OLC surface increase after the reaction due to oxidation by O2 and chloroplatinic acid. The Pt nanoparticles show (111) plane lattice fringes of face-centered cubic Pt with coordination number of ~6.6 (estimated particle size ~1 nm by EXAFS).
